title: Computable lower bound of the parameterized entanglement monotone
authors: Ning Yang, Yu Guo, Shuanping Du
abstract: Although numerous measures of entanglement have been proposed so far, the calculation of a given faithful entanglement measure is a hard work since it is always involved in some optimization process. It is, therefore, important to estimate the lower bound of a given entanglement measure for an arbitrary quantum state. This results in a subject of intensive mathematical research. In particular, along this line, the lower bounds of concurrence or other measures that are induced from concurrence have been explored a lot. Here, we investigate the lower bounds of two kinds of entanglement monotones, i.e., $q$-concurrence ($q>1$) and $α$-concurrence ($0<α<1$), or termed the parameterized entanglement monotone together. We obtain, in the light of the informationally complete ($N$, $M$)-positive operator-valued measure [($N$, $M$)-POVM], the lower bounds for the case of $\frac12<α<1$, $1<q<2$ for two-qudit states, and the case of $2\leqslant q<3$ for two-qubit states. We list several examples which show that the lower bounds based on ($N$, $M$)-POVM outperform that of GSIC-POVM and SIC-POVM, and all these measurement based bounds are better then the ones induced by positive partial transpose (PPT) and realignment criteria in literature. In addition, we obtain an analytical formula of the parameterized entanglement monotone with $\frac12<α<1$ and $1<q<2$ for the isotropic state.
summary: This paper develops computable lower bounds for a class of parameterized entanglement monotones, specifically q-concurrence and alpha-concurrence. By using a generalized (N, M)-POVM framework, the authors provide bounds that are tighter than standard PPT and realignment criteria. The work also includes analytical expressions for these measures in the case of isotropic states.
main_problem: The difficulty of calculating faithful entanglement measures due to complex optimization processes leads to the need for computable lower bounds for parameterized entanglement monotones.
main_result: The authors derive new, tighter lower bounds for q-concurrence and alpha-concurrence using (N, M)-POVMs, which outperform existing bounds from PPT, realignment, and SIC-POVM criteria.
method: The study employs informationally complete (N, M)-positive operator-valued measures, Lagrange multipliers for optimization, and convexity/concavity arguments to extend results from pure to mixed states.
model_or_system: The research focuses on bipartite quantum states (two-qubit and two-qudit systems) and specifically analyzes isotropic states.
important_parameters: Dimension d, power parameters q and alpha, and POVM parameters N and M.
main_assumptions: The derivation assumes specific parameter regimes (e.g., 1 < q < 2, 1/2 < alpha < 1) and the existence of a twirling operator to reduce states to isotropic form.
figures_summary: Figures compare the proposed lower bounds against existing criteria (alpha-concurrence and q-concurrence) for pure and isotropic states, demonstrating the increased tightness of the new bounds.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of entanglement estimation, defines the parameterized monotones, introduces the (N, M)-POVM framework, provides theorems for new lower bounds, compares performance against existing criteria, and derives analytical formulas for isotropic states.
why_interesting: This is relevant for quantum information theorists as it provides more efficient, measurement-based tools for quantifying entanglement in bipartite systems without requiring full state tomography or complex optimizations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
